zh.lifeSummary: 當Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain於大約1310年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾時，他的父親Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain22歲，母親Joan verch Ieuan20歲。他和Margaret verch Gwrwared至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain于大约1310年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾时，他的父亲Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain22岁，母亲Joan verch Ieuan20岁。他和Margaret verch Gwrwared至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Owain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened form of   O'Callaghan  .

variants.name: O'Callaghan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Dermot, Donal, Kiernan, Niall, Conor, Donovan, John Patrick, Kieran, Siobhan, Tiernan.
Irish (Cork): from Gaelic Ó Ceallacháin, see Callahan .

nameMeanings.name: Llewelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

variants.name: Llywellyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

variants.name: Llywelyn
meanings: (Welsh)  See  Llewelyn .

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: See Lywelyn.

nameMeanings.name: ddu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Du
meanings: ‘Wealthy’.

variants.name: Dau
meanings: From Old Norse dagr ‘day’.

nameMeanings.name: Black
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blagg
meanings: English: variant of   Black   with voicing of the final consonant.

variants.name: Blake
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has two quite distinct etymologies. It is both from Old English blæc ‘black’ and from Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ it was thus originally a nickname given to someone with hair or skin that was either remarkably dark or remarkably light. It is now quite popular as a boy's name.

variants.name: Blackie
meanings: Scottish: diminutive of   Black  .
English: nickname for a person with dark eyes, from Middle English blak ‘black’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English blæc + ēage).

variants.name: Blackson
meanings: English: patronymic from the nickname or surname Black + -son. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Block
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a nickname for someone resembling a badger (Middle English broc(k), Old English brocc, of Celtic origin).

variants.name: Blacklock
meanings: English (northern): nickname for someone with dark hair, from Middle English blāk + lok(e) (Old English blæc + locc), although blāk might mean either ‘dark’ or ‘fair’ (see Black ). The meaning ‘dark hair’ is the most probable since this name contrasts with Whitlock .

variants.name: Blatch
meanings: English (Essex, but historically also in south and southwest England): nickname from Middle English blache, bleche ‘pale, pallid, of fair complexion’ (Old English blǣce).

variants.name: Blackett
meanings: English:
nickname for a person with dark or fair hair, from Middle English blak ‘black’ (Old English blæc) or ‘fair’ (Old English blāc) + hed, heved ‘head’. See also Black .
nickname from Middle English blak ‘black’ + hod ‘hood’.

en.lifeSummary: When Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain was born about 1310, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain, was 22 and his mother, Joan verch Ieuan, was 20. He had at least 1 son with Margaret verch Gwrwared.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain est né environ 1310, à Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Joan verch Ieuan, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret verch Gwrwared.

de.lifeSummary: Als Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain um 1310 in Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain 22 und seine Mutter Joan verch Ieuan 20. Er hatte mit Margaret verch Gwrwared mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain è nato il circa 1310 nel Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Joan verch Ieuan, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margaret verch Gwrwared.

ja.Carmarthenshire, Wales: ウェールズカーマースエンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owainが約1310年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owainは22歳，母Joan verch Ieuanは20歳であった。彼はMargaret verch Gwrwaredとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain(이)가 대략 1310년 웨일스 카마테실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan verch Ieuan(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Margaret verch Gwrwared(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Joan verch Ieuan, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Margaret verch Gwrwared.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain родился около 1310 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Joan verch Ieuan, 20. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Margaret verch Gwrwared.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Llewelyn ddu Black ap Owain nació aproximadamente 1310, en Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Owain ap Gruffydd ap Owain, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Joan verch Ieuan, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Margaret verch Gwrwared.
